commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: White Pass Railroad and Klondike Highway - Multilingual
commerceProducts.description: This tour lets you experience two different sides of White Pass while enjoying a narrated commentary in your native language. First, you'll drive along Klondike Highway, one of North America's most scenic roads, through West White Pass in the St. Elias Range. Then, board a vintage railcar and retrace the "Trail of '98" as you journey down the other side of the canyon. Enjoy spectacular views of mountains, glaciers, and waterfalls along the way.
highlights: Drive parallel to the historic Gold Rush Trail of '98 as you travel up Klondike Highway through the West White Pass.
Ride the famous White Pass & Yukon Railroad through the opposite side of the Pass and down the canyon.
Learn about the places you visit in the language of your choosing using a GPS-activated narration.

activityLevel.description: Full participation may require leisurely walking over primarily even surfaces. There may be a limited number of steps, a minimal amount of cobblestones or uneven surfaces, and some standing for extended periods of time.

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Great Alaskan Lumberjack Show and Axe Throwing Challenge
commerceProducts.description: Celebrate southeast Alaska's rich logging history at the rip-roaring good time offered by the Great Alaskan Lumberjack, where premiere woodsmen compete to be the "bull of the woods" and you get to throw an axe without any legal or moral ramifications.
highlights: A quick walk brings you to the Great Alaskan Lumberjack Show, where Alaska's frontier lumberjacks face off against their British Columbian counterparts in a competition featuring chopping, sawing, axe throwing, log rolling and a 50-foot tree climb, all undergirded by the Last Frontier's long and proud longing history.

Know which arm is your axe-throwing arm? You're going to find out as, after a tutorial from an expert, you compete against your fellow tour guests in a friendly competition throwing a five-pound, double-bit axe at a target.
Enjoy the fun and ambiance of this Ketchikan cultural event, and walk back to the port to meet your awaiting ship at your leisure.

activityLevel.description: This tour is not recommended for guests with pre-existing medical conditions such as cardiac, respiratory, or orthopedic conditions, including joint injuries or disease, or any other serious medical condition. Guests must consider their physical fitness level and medical history when determining whether this tour is appropriate. commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Rainforest Hike and Brew

promotion.title: CEL Shorex Evergreen Destination Highlights 60-79% booked

commerceProducts.description: Enjoy an exhilarating hike as you learn about and experience the world's largest temperate rainforest before toasting your accomplishment with a cold beer at a popular, local bar.
highlights: Begin your Alaskan adventure with a scenic drive through historic Ketchikan to the trailhead, with your friendly guide sharing local knowledge of the history and culture of the people of Ketchikan.
Enjoy a narrated hike through the Tongass National Forest, the world's largest temperate rainforest, and gaze at the giant cedar, hemlock, and spruce trees towering overhead.
Follow the stone steps to a lookout featuring beautiful views of a cascading waterfall.
Cool off with a local brew at Arctic Bar, the oldest bar still operating in Ketchikan.

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Rainforest Bike and Nature Walk

promotion.title: CEL Shorex Evergreen Destination Highlights 60-79% booked

commerceProducts.description: Bike through the raw, untouched beauty of the Tongass National Forest, an Alaskan gem and one of the last intact temperate rainforests in the world.
highlights: A short shuttle ride brings you to the outfitting station to gear up and join your experienced guide on a three-mile biking path through the towering beauty of Tongass National Forest.

Pedal through the immense Alaska beauty, with Sitka spruce and western hemlock high above as you ride through the largest national forest in the U.S., home to rain-soaked mosses, Pacific silver fir, and lichens as well as Sitka black-tailed deer and majestic bald eagles.

Step off your bike at the picturesque Connell Lake Dam that supported the logging industry at Ward Cove Pulp Mill, and continue on a nature walk along a stream popular amongst salmon during spawning season.
Settle in for a fireside snack under the canopy of beautiful, coniferous trees before saddling back up for the return ride along the pipeline trail, taking in the natural sights.

activityLevel.description: Full participation may require leisurely walking over primarily even surfaces. There may be a limited number of steps, a minimal amount of cobblestones or uneven surfaces, and some standing for extended periods of time.
activityLevel.name: Mild

source.altText: group of people with sled dogs.

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Musher's Camp and Gold Panning
commerceProducts.description: Your journey begins with an entertaining historic tour from your knowledgeable guide through downtown Skagway and along nine miles of stunning coastline to our Adventure Camp in Dyea. Kick off your visit with what we like to call a “Musher Talk.” We have been sharing our passion for dog sledding for years, and in that time, we have gathered up the most frequently asked questions about the sport. In the Musher Talk, we will share the fascinating answers and welcome any additional questions you may have. Of course, we make sure you have plenty of time to meet some of the actual athletes who do these amazing things, our sled dogs! After that, have a chance to pan for gold in our riverside sluice! Once your guide shows you the art of gold panning, you can test your luck to see what you can find. Then you will have time to browse the gift shop and relax in our saloon with a deck overlooking the picturesque stream.
highlights: Enjoy a scenic coach journey through downtown Skagway, viewing nine miles of breathtaking Alaska coastline.
Learn about Alaskan dog mushing and meet the sled dogs.
Your guide will show you the art of gold panning, test your luck to see what you can find.

activityLevel.description: Full participation may require leisurely walking over primarily even surfaces. There may be a limited number of steps, a minimal amount of cobblestones or uneven surfaces, and some standing for extended periods of time.
activityLevel.name: Mild

Man teaching cooking class holding a cooking book.

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Kitchen Science Alaska Garden to Table Food Tour
commerceProducts.description: Experience a piece of American history – and cuisine - with a visit to the historic Skagway Inn, where you mingle with local Alaskans, immerse yourself in centuries of tradition and discover the culinary secrets of this charming rural community.
highlights: After a brief tour through the city of Skagway, meet with the chef and owner of the historic Skagway Inn and embark on a behind-the-scenes tour of the facilities, as you discover the science that transforms garden-grown ingredients into delicious meals.

Stroll through the inn’s culinary garden and learn about the foods and herbs native to Alaska, along with the techniques used to catch and smoke fresh local fish. Then, head inside the inn, which once served as a brothel during the Klondike Gold Rush.

Here, learn the science behind making a loaf of sourdough bread, as you feed the establishment’s sourdough starter and savor some organic veggies, before transforming simple ingredients from the garden into a sweet concoction – your very own homemade ice cream.

Finish your culinary journey with a hint of luxury, as you enjoy a golden, non-alcoholic drink - a tasty reminder of the town's gold rush past - before returning to your awaiting ship with a few new recipes and skills to bring back home.

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Talbot Lake Rainforest Hike
commerceProducts.description: Explore the last frontier on an amazing trek through the Tongass National Forest, one of the last intact temperate rainforests in the world, during a hike that invites you to immerse yourself in the immense, unique beauty of Alaska.
highlights: A short drive delivers you into the heart of the Alaskan wilderness and a leisurely hike among the giant coniferous trees of the Tongass National Forest, the largest national forest in the United States.

Walk over the moss-covered grounds of the old-growth forest and listen to the trickle of flowing streams, punctuated by the calls of dozens of birds chirping above as your guide narrates the story of this illustrious natural habitat.
Arrive at the scenic Talbot Lake, where a warm campfire set against the natural backdrop of the surrounding forest awaits.
Recharge with a snack during a quick break, tighten those laces for the return hike to the trail entrance before boarding your shuttle for the return drive to the pier.

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Ghost and Goodtime Girls Walking Tour
commerceProducts.description:
Join a madam from the Red Onion Saloon for a fun and informative walking tour through downtown Skagway!
First, you’ll be greeted at the pier by a lavishly costumed and highly entertaining Madam. After a quick shuttle transfer to town, she will guide you through the back alleys and former red-light districts of Skagway. Here, women of the evening brushed shoulders with notorious characters, wide-eyed stampeders, and dangerous odds. Stories of Klondike Kate, Cad Wilson and Diamond Lil depict the adventurous stories of the women who stampeded north. You will pass historic and haunted gold rush buildings as you learn of Skagway’s rich history and the women who helped make it what it is.
Your experience culminates with a stop at the world-famous Red Onion Saloon’s restored Brothel Museum. Here, join in a champagne toast and explore the hallways and bedrooms of this celebrated brothel, observing original relics and images of the ladies who worked there. Bring your camera and you may even catch a glimpse of the resident ghost, Lydia!
*Your tour concludes at the Red Onion Saloon and guests may choose to stay in town and explore more of Skagway or take the short walk back to the pier.
highlights: Visit the Red Onion Saloon and Brothel Museum and see the artifacts left behind by the working women over 125 years ago.
Explore Skagway's back streets and be entertained by your masterful guide and Madam on this walking tour that's been featured on podcasts, ghost hunting shows, and even the Discovery channel.

activityLevel.description: Full participation may require leisurely walking over primarily even surfaces. There may be a limited number of steps, a minimal amount of cobblestones or uneven surfaces, and some standing for extended periods of time.

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Ketchikan Pub Crawl
commerceProducts.description: Let's raise a glass to history! This fun, informative and engaging 90-minute walking tour is sure to please anyone who wants to really get to know the local culture of Ketchikan. You'll embark on your pub crawl with a true Alaskan local who will share the colorful history of the bar scene through stories and vintage photographs. You'll hear infamous stories of days gone by, facts of the prohibition era when the red-light district was in full swing and the legacy of many of Ketchikan’s most popular hot spots. You’ll enjoy an adult beverage at each stop, stroll down historic streets and get a real sense of what life is like in this unique rainforest town. Join us and belly up to the bar in Ketchikan!
highlights: Meet your local guide and set out to explore some of Ketchikan's favorite hot spots.
Hear stories of the Prohibition era and Ketchikan's Red Light District .

activityLevel.description: Full participation may require extended periods of walking over even and uneven surfaces, steep terrain and/or water activity in a slight current. There may be steps, inclines, cobblestone surfaces, and extended periods of standing. Participants with physical limitations should take this into account.
activityLevel.name: Moderate

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Klondike Summit, Gold Panning and Prospectors Lunch

promotion.title: CEL Shorex Evergreen Destination Highlights 60-79% booked

commerceProducts.description: Discover Skagway's rich gold-mining history as you traverse the scenic Klondike Highway aboard a comfortable coach, viewing historic landmarks and vestiges of Gold Rush days along the way. Then meet vivacious pioneer characters, where you'll also try your hand at gold panning. Linger over an open-air rustic feast as you approach a tasty spread of Alderwood-grilled WILD Alaska salmon, Cheechako chicken, Chilkoot baked beans, Tongass wild-rice pilaf, a selection of sides, salads, and beverages. Eat some blueberry cake for dessert as a sweet finish.
highlights: Gaze in awe at majesty of the Alaskan wilderness as you journey across the Klondike Highway.
Follow in the footsteps of old-time gold seekers as you pan for the precious metal.
Learn about Skagway's role in the Gold Rush, an ear when many risked everything in hopes of riches.

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: To The Summit!
commerceProducts.description: Adventure to the 3,292 foot Summit of White Pass by mini-bus in just 1.5 hours Step off at scenic viewpoints for breathtaking photos! Your mountain guide will pass on a century of stories. You will see Dead Horse Trail, the White Pass Railroad, Pitchfork Falls, Moore bridge and numerous glaciers and peaks during your personalized, group experience. Stop at the Summit and and enjoy magnificent views of the scenic mountain landscape before returning to Skagway through USCBP. Upon returning to Skagway , a stop is made in town for guests wishing to shop or explore independently.
highlights: Discover Alaska's spectacular White Pass Summit, via the Klondike Highway, known as the original Gold Rush Trail of' '98.
Small group experience with local guide,
See Skagway and White Pass in just 1.5 hour, combines well with other tours

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Adventure Hike Skagway
commerceProducts.description: Join experienced guides on an exciting hiking adventure through the Tongass Rainforest that immerses you in the incredible beauty of Southeast Alaska. Your adventure will begin with a guided driving town tour of Skagway and continue up the White Pass on the Klondike Highway. Along the way your guide will regale you with history about the Klondike Gold Rush and the natural history of the area. From the trailhead you will spend the next two hours hiking through the beautiful alpine forest surrounded by the towering snow-capped mountains of the White Pass. Your naturalist guides will stop frequently along the way to share their knowledge about the native flora, fauna, and to describe the amazing glacial history of Alaska. Each group is outfitted with binoculars to maximize your opportunities to see up close the spectacular mountain and river scenery. Midway through the hike you will be treated to a delicious snack at the final breathtaking overlook with incredible views of the Skagway valley, White Pass, and the Lynn Canal.
highlights: Drive through the town of Skagway en route to Alaska's famous White Pass, where your trailhead begins.
Spend approximately 2 hours hiking through the alpine rainforest, surrounded by the snowcapped mountains of the White Pass.
Experience the Tongass National Forest, the nation’s largest rainforest.
Learn about the native flora, fauna, and the glacial history of Alaska
Breathtaking overlooks with beautiful views
